repositories.prompt_note: Use the ZeroHrs monorepo source tree and current main branch. Feedback-created issues come from mobile users and should be reviewed conservatively: if labels/body/admin metadata indicate a feedback report, treat it as an external user report even when the GitHub issue author is a ZeroHrs maintainer or repository owner. Verify the issue against current code, prefer Android Crabbox proof for mobile UI/runtime claims, keep issues open unless current main clearly implements the requested behavior, and create implementation PRs only after a maintainer command. Android proof must read test credentials from configured secrets instead of hardcoded prompts, docs, or scripts. When the app login flow is needed, use ZEROHRS_TEST_EMAIL and ZEROHRS_TEST_PASSWORD from the environment without printing or committing their raw values. Missing local adb, emulator, or ffmpeg on the GitHub Actions runner is not a proof blocker; Android proof must run on the configured Crabbox SSH runner via the target repo proof wrapper or an issue-specific crabbox run command. During planning/review, collect current-state reproduction artifacts only; there is no after-fix proof yet. Planning/review comments must include or link the current-state Android screenshot(s) and MP4 recording(s) captured by manually navigating the real app UI; do not treat launcher or generic start-page screenshots as sufficient behavior proof. During implementation, the executor agent decides the issue-specific reproduction path by manually navigating the real app UI with adb, emulator controls, or an external temporary proof driver and saves before/after loading screenshots and MP4 recordings under reports/clawsweeper/android-proof inside the target checkout for ClawSweeper to collect and publish; before media must be from the pre-fix current-main checkout and after media must be from the fixed branch/PR head, never same-checkout early/later screenshots renamed as before/after proof. Do not add product code, navigation routes, screens, env flags, Constants.expoConfig proof plumbing, EXPO_PUBLIC_*PROOF* variables, zeroHrsProofRoute/proofRoute files, or tests whose only purpose is proof navigation to expose a proof-only route; proof drivers belong outside the committed app diff or in ignored report artifacts. Required execution media include before-loading.png, before.mp4, after-loading.png, after.mp4, before.png, after.png, command.log, emulator.log, app.log, and proof-manifest.json with structured captures metadata. Completed proof manifests must include distinct before/after refs, captures.before.issue_reproduced=true, non-empty captures.before.issue_evidence, captures.after.issue_resolved=true, and non-empty captures.after.fix_evidence. If Crabbox returns .crabbox/runs artifacts, copy the proof files back into reports/clawsweeper/android-proof before returning; external /tmp-only proof is not publishable. Do not edit shared proof harness files for one issue: scripts/crabbox/android-proof.sh, scripts/crabbox/run-android-proof.sh, their tests, and docs/crabbox-hetzner-feedback.md are protected infrastructure and ClawSweeper restores them before staging. Put issue-specific navigation in a temporary proof driver, inline Crabbox command, or reports/clawsweeper/android-proof artifact instead of shared scripts or target app source/tests. When feedback depends on account, app, billing, referral, entitlement, or database state, seed or mock data in the local dev/test database to reproduce the issue; create and use local admin/referral codes or local trial/plan state when needed to pass billing/onboarding gates, document the code type and seed commands, and never use production data.

Use the ClawHub source tree and current main branch. Review every issue and PR with the same evidence standard. Propose auto-close for issues and pull requests that are certainly implemented on main; for pull requests only, also allow age-gated mostly implemented on main. Keep everything else open.

Use the ClawSweeper source tree and current main branch. Review bot automation, workflow, and documentation changes conservatively. Propose auto-close for issues and pull requests that are certainly implemented on main; for pull requests only, also allow age-gated mostly implemented on main. Keep everything else open for maintainer triage.

Use the fs-safe source tree and current main branch. Review filesystem-safety, path-handling, and package changes conservatively. Propose auto-close for issues and pull requests that are certainly implemented on main; for pull requests only, also allow age-gated mostly implemented on main. Keep everything else open for maintainer triage.

generic_fallbacks.prompt_note: Use the {target_repo} source tree and current default branch. This repository is using the generic ZeroHrs onboarding profile, so review conservatively, prefer source-backed and Crabbox-backed evidence, and do not auto-close issues unless current main clearly implements the requested behavior.

Use the {target_repo} source tree and current default branch. This repository is using the generic OpenClaw onboarding profile, so keep review conservative: issues and pull requests may be auto-closed only when the exact change is certainly already implemented on the default branch; pull requests may also be age-gated mostly implemented there.

Use the {target_repo} source tree and current default branch. This repository is using the generic personal-repository onboarding profile, so review conservatively and do not auto-close issues or pull requests unless a repository-specific profile later enables that policy.
